news.title: Complete pending stations on Pune metro line III, meet March 2026 deadline: CM to PMRDA
news.para: Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis has directed PMRDA to expedite work on the pending 13 stations of the Hinjewadi–Shivajinagar Metro Line 3, aiming for a March 2026 completion. While 90% of the work is done, IT industry representatives are pushing for a phased opening to provide immediate commuter relief.

news.title: Over 160 illegal PGs found in Bengaluru east, civic body mandates compliance by Nov 5
news.para: Bengaluru East city corporation has launched a crackdown on 165 unlicensed paying guest accommodations, issuing a seven-day ultimatum for owners to obtain trade licenses and comply with safety norms. Violations include operating in residential zones, lacking fire safety, poor hygiene, and no CCTV. A new certification system is proposed to ensure regulated and safe PG accommodations for residents.

news.title: Ghaziabad development body to draft a master plan for 61 villages near expressways
news.para: Ghaziabad Development Authority (GDA) will create a separate master plan for 61 villages along the Delhi Meerut Expressway and Eastern Peripheral Expressway to regulate growth. These villages, previously governed by gram panchayats, are experiencing exponential and unregulated development due to the expressways.

news.title: Maharashtra regularises land parcels restricted under fragmentation act
news.para: Maharashtra government has regularised lakhs of land parcels previously restricted under the Fragmentation and Consolidation of Holdings Act, 1947. This historic reform, effective November 3, 2025, benefits nearly two crore citizens by officially recording their names on 7/12 land extracts for non-agricultural lands, resolving decades of confusion and hardship.

news.title: Hyderabad: Case against construction firm for duping nine flat buyers of over ₹4 crore
news.para: Cyberabad Economic Offences Wing has filed a cheating case against a Jubilee Hills construction firm for allegedly defrauding nine buyers of ₹4.41 crore. Investors were promised a stalled Tellapur project with early bird rates and buy-back options. Despite symbolic ground-breaking, approvals and construction never commenced, leading to project cancellation and demands for refunds.

news.title: Amitabh Bachchan sells two luxury apartments in Mumbai for ₹12 crore
news.para: Amitabh Bachchan has sold two adjacent luxury apartments in Mumbai's Goregaon for a combined Rs 12 crore. The 3,640 sq ft property, located on the 47th floor, was sold in two separate deals. Each apartment included two car parking spaces.

news.title: Registrars, sub-registrars have no authority to issue NOC to housing societies for redevelopment
news.para: In a pivotal directive, Maharashtra's Cooperation Commissioner Deepak Taware has underscored that registrars and sub-registrars do not possess the power to issue No Objection Certificates for the redevelopment of housing societies. Instead, the ultimate authority resides with the cooperative housing society's general body, marking a significant step towards clarity in the state's redevelopment processes.

news.title: Nexus Select Trust's NOI up 14% to ₹425 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Nexus Select Trust experienced a robust growth of 14% in net operating income, climbing to Rs 424.9 crore for the second quarter. The company announced a generous distribution of Rs 333 crore to its stakeholders.

news.title: Brookfield India REIT plans to acquire 7.7 million sq ft business park for over ₹13,000 crore
news.para: Brookfield India Real Estate Trust is on the verge of enhancing its business landscape with a notable asset acquisition in Bengaluru. By committing Rs 13,125 crore for 7.7 million square feet, BIRET is making its mark in a major office hub, amplifying its REIT size by more than a third.

news.title: Geberit wary on European new-build construction
news.para: Swiss plumbing powerhouse Geberit has made headlines by bumping up its annual sales projection, now anticipating a 4.5% growth in sales. This adjustment is a direct result of stronger-than-expected results during the third quarter. The company believes that the renovation sector will drive growth, while acknowledging that new construction projects across Europe pose ongoing challenges.

news.title: SC sets aside order directing removal of illegal constructions in Gurugram's DLF City
news.para: The Supreme Court has overturned a High Court directive to demolish illegal constructions in Gurugram's DLF City. This decision came after the apex court found that property owners were not given a chance to be heard. The Supreme Court emphasized that justice requires fair hearing for all parties.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh RERA removes 22 projects from 'abeyance list'
news.para: Uttar Pradesh RERA has lifted 22 stalled real estate projects from its 'abeyance list', allowing construction to resume on 8,856 units. This decision aims to provide relief to homebuyers and boost the state's economy by reviving pending projects that faced issues with incomplete documentation.

news.title: Berger Paints' profit down 23% to ₹206 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: For the September quarter of FY26, Berger Paints India reported a steep net profit drop of over 23%, largely attributed to a slump in exterior product sales coupled with heightened investment in brand promotion. While revenue from operations crept up marginally, demand was notably affected by prolonged monsoon rains.

news.title: Indore: Portions of 11 houses demolished in Guru Govind Colony
news.para: Indore Municipal Corporation demolished front portions of 11 residential structures and its own waste transfer station boundary wall to facilitate road widening under the Master Plan. The action, aimed at easing traffic congestion and preparing for Simhastha 2028, followed prior notices to residents. The IMC is also filing a caveat in the high court regarding 23 roads slated for widening.

news.title: Lucknow development body extends deadline for Atal Nagar housing scheme
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority has extended the registration deadline for its Atal Nagar Housing Scheme to November 21, offering 2,496 1-BHK and 2-BHK flats for low and middle-income groups. Meanwhile, the Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel Housing Scheme in Dalibagh, featuring 72 flats on land recovered from illegal possession, saw an overwhelming response and concluded its registration on November 3.

news.title: Noida airport nearly ready but survey finds 94% business plots along Yamuna expressway unused
news.para: A government survey reveals a significant portion of industrial land along the Yamuna Expressway remains undeveloped, with 94% of plots vacant just months before the Noida International Airport's launch. This contrasts sharply with Noida's robust industrial occupancy, highlighting stalled growth potential in the YEIDA region despite rising demand.

news.title: Qualcomm India leases 2.56 lakh sq ft office space in Bengaluru
news.para: Qualcomm India has leased a significant office space in Bengaluru. This expansion adds to the technology giant's growing presence in India. The new lease at Constellation Business Park is for five years. This move highlights the strong demand for large office spaces by tech firms in India.  Qualcomm continues to invest in key tech hubs across the country.

news.title: Gujarat plans five new satellite towns to ease urban expansion
news.para: Gujarat's government is transforming five towns—Kalol, Sanand, Savli, Bardoli, and Hirasar—into fully developed satellite towns around its major cities. A high-level committee will oversee infrastructure development to ensure synchronized growth, aiming to ease pressure on urban centers and foster balanced development and investment.

news.title: Mumbai: Court rejects anticipatory bail plea of builder after death at construction site
news.para: A Mumbai court denied a builder anticipatory bail after a young woman died when a concrete brick fell from his under-construction building. Photographs revealed torn safety nets and a lack of pedestrian protection, violating safety guidelines. The judge noted the builder concealed a prior bail application, indicating he did not approach the court with clean hands.

news.title: Consumer court orders Nagpur builder to refund homebuyer's money with interest
news.para: A consumer commission has ordered a real estate firm to refund Rs3 lakh with 9% interest to a doctor couple, along with Rs30,000 for harassment and Rs10,000 for costs. The firm was found deficient in service for retaining the booking amount after cancellation, deeming it unjust enrichment without a forfeiture clause.
